answer.markdown:
## AgentsKit Chat dogfood

How Ask Playbook composes the public AgentsKit Chat framework without recreating chat state.

Type: **Guide**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/agentskit-chat)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/agentskit-chat.md)

## Contribute a production-earned pattern

Turn a reproducible engineering lesson into a reviewable Playbook pattern, prompt, template, or gate.

Type: **Guide**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/contributing)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/contributing.mdx)

## Local-first pattern discovery

See which Playbook questions answer instantly in the browser and when repository-specific synthesis uses the backend.

Type: **Guide**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/discovery)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/discovery.mdx)

## For agents

The shortest reliable path for an agent to retrieve, apply, and verify the Agents Playbook.

Type: **Guide**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/for-agents)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/for-agents.mdx)

## Getting started

Adopt the playbook in your project in under an hour.

Type: **Guide**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/getting-started)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/getting-started.mdx)

## Glossary

Short definitions for the terms used across this playbook.

Type: **Reference**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/glossary)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/glossary.md)

## Welcome

The open engineering harness for coding agents — train repeatable behavior, not model weights.

Type: **Index**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/index.mdx)

## The matrix — pillars × phases

This is the master content map. Each cell points to the practices that apply when pillar meets SDLC phase.

Type: **Reference**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/matrix)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/matrix.md)

## Onboard Your Agent

One paste that feeds any coding agent the whole playbook and has it audit your repo — Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Codex, Copilot, and more.

Type: **Guide**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/onboard-your-agent)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/onboard-your-agent.md)

## Phase 01 — Discover

Define what you are building, who consumes it, and what success looks like — before agents touch the codebase.

Type: **SDLC Phase**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/phases/01-discover)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/phases/01-discover/index.md)

## Phase 02 — Design

Turn the discover brief into ADRs, RFCs, and a contract package skeleton the build phase can compose against.

Type: **SDLC Phase**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/phases/02-design)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/phases/02-design/index.md)

## Phase 03 — Build

Where most of the agent-augmented work happens. Discipline shifts from ''writing code'' to ''stating intent, then verifying output against it''.

Type: **SDLC Phase**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/phases/03-build)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/phases/03-build/index.md)

## Phase 04 — Test

How ''tests pass'' stops being a feeling and starts being a contract.

Type: **SDLC Phase**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/phases/04-test)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/phases/04-test/index.md)

## Phase 05 — Ship

How to turn a green main into a release without surprising consumers.

Type: **SDLC Phase**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/phases/05-ship)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/phases/05-ship/index.md)

## Phase 06 — Operate

What ''running it'' looks like after agents have shipped the first release.

Type: **SDLC Phase**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/phases/06-operate)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/phases/06-operate/index.md)

## Agent Compatibility Pattern

One playbook, every agent — how to map the bootstrap-doc convention onto Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, Codex, and the rest.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/agent-compatibility-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/agent-compatibility-pattern.md)

## Bootstrap Doc Pattern

The file an agent reads first, every session. Two files together: one for non-negotiables, one for routing.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/bootstrap-doc-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/bootstrap-doc-pattern.md)

## Concurrent Agent Pattern

How to survive — and benefit from — multiple agents working in the same repo at the same time.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/concurrent-agent-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/concurrent-agent-pattern.md)

## Context Management Pattern

Engineer what goes into the context window — selection, ordering, compaction, retention — because the context is the program the model runs.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/context-management-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/context-management-pattern.md)

## Hallucination Reduction Pattern

Make model output you can put your name on — grounding, constrained generation, verification, and abstention — so the system says "I don''t know" instead of confidently inventing.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/hallucination-reduction-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/hallucination-reduction-pattern.md)

## Honest Confidence Pattern

How to report done-ness so a human can trust it — separating what was automatically verified from what was merely claimed.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/honest-confidence-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/honest-confidence-pattern.md)

## Human-in-the-Loop Pattern

Design where humans review, approve, correct, and take over from agents — so autonomy scales with confidence and every correction becomes training signal.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/human-in-the-loop-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/human-in-the-loop-pattern.md)

## Pillar — AI Collaboration

How to make an agent productive in your repo on day one and durably good across sessions.

Type: **Pillar**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/index.md)

## Memory Pattern

How to make agents durably learn from prior sessions without polluting context with chat transcripts.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/memory-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/memory-pattern.md)

## Open Knowledge Format Pattern

Represent agent-readable knowledge as a directory of markdown + YAML frontmatter — the OKF open standard, and how it formalizes patterns this playbook already uses.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/open-knowledge-format-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/open-knowledge-format-pattern.md)

## Prompt Versioning Pattern

Treat prompts as versioned, tested, experimentable production assets — not strings buried in code — so you can A/B them, measure impact, and roll back instantly.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/prompt-versioning-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/prompt-versioning-pattern.md)

## Self-Describe Pattern

Ship a machine-readable manifest of what your system can do, so external agents can discover and call it without reading your source.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/self-describe-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/self-describe-pattern.md)

## Slash Commands Pattern

How to turn repeated workflows into palette-invoked commands so they run identically every time.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/slash-commands-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/slash-commands-pattern.md)

## Sub-agent Pattern

How to delegate scoped tasks to specialist agents so the orchestrator stays focused.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/sub-agent-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/sub-agent-pattern.md)

## Tool & Capability Design Pattern

Design the abstraction layer between a model and your product — tools, file systems, artifacts, skills — so complex multi-step work feels natural to the model and stays reliable for users.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/tool-design-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/tool-design-pattern.md)

## AI Collaboration — Universal Principles

How to get production-grade work out of an AI coding agent, durably, across sessions and across multiple agents working in parallel.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/universal)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/universal.md)

## Verify-First Pattern

Why an agent must trust live code over docs, plans, and its own memory — and how to verify before claiming or "fixing".

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/ai-collaboration/verify-first-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/ai-collaboration/verify-first-pattern.md)

## ADR Pattern

How to record architecture decisions so future agents (and humans) can find them, trust them, and supersede them cleanly.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/adr-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/adr-pattern.md)

## Anti-Overengineering

How to keep agents (and engineers) from building three layers of abstraction for what would have been ten lines of code.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/anti-overengineering)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/anti-overengineering.md)

## API Gateway Pattern

The edge between clients and your services — what belongs there, what doesn''t, and how to keep it from becoming a monolith in disguise.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/api-gateway-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/api-gateway-pattern.md)

## API Versioning + Deprecation Pattern

How to evolve public contracts without surprising consumers.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/api-versioning-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/api-versioning-pattern.md)

## Caching + CDN Pattern

How to layer caches so the system is fast, cheap, and consistent — three properties at tension.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/caching-cdn-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/caching-cdn-pattern.md)

values: playbook Contracts — Zod Method Registry Pattern

answer.markdown:
## Contracts — Zod Method Registry Pattern

TS-concrete recipe for a typed JSON-RPC / HTTP / IPC boundary. Scales to several hundred methods across dozens of namespaces in a real production codebase.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/contracts-zod-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/contracts-zod-pattern.md)

## Distributed Data Pattern

How to design data layout when one database stops being enough — read replicas, sharding, replication lag, CAP trade-offs, eventual consistency.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/distributed-data-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/distributed-data-pattern.md)

## Error Hierarchy

How to design an error model that survives multi-agent development and client-side pattern matching.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/error-hierarchy)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/error-hierarchy.md)

## Event Streaming Pattern

How to design async, decoupled communication via queues, pub/sub, and event streams — without losing events, double-processing, or stalling consumers.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/event-streaming-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/event-streaming-pattern.md)

## Feature Flags Pattern

How to ship code separately from shipping behavior, without accumulating a flag graveyard.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/feature-flags-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/feature-flags-pattern.md)

## File-size Budget

How to keep files reviewable when agents write most of the code.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/file-size-budget)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/file-size-budget.md)

## Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Pattern

How to define + version + review + apply infrastructure as code, instead of clicking around cloud consoles.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/iac-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/iac-pattern.md)

## Pillar — Architecture

How to keep a codebase legible and modular under multi-agent development.

Type: **Pillar**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/index.md)

## Multi-Region Pattern

How to operate across geographic regions for latency, availability, and data sovereignty — without losing your mind.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/multi-region-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/multi-region-pattern.md)

## Offline-First + Sync Pattern

How to design apps that work without network connectivity and reconcile state when connectivity returns.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/offline-first-sync-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/offline-first-sync-pattern.md)

values: Platform Engineering + Internal Developer Platform Pattern
playbook Platform Engineering + Internal Developer Platform Pattern

answer.markdown:
## Platform Engineering + Internal Developer Platform Pattern

How to build the layer between cloud / infra and product engineers, so product teams ship fast without re-learning infra each time.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/platform-engineering-idp-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/platform-engineering-idp-pattern.md)

citations.title: Platform Engineering + Internal Developer Platform Pattern

answer.markdown:
## RFC Pattern

When the decision is bigger than your team. Used for changes to public contracts, wire formats, plugin protocols, and any breaking change a consumer outside this repo would notice.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/rfc-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/rfc-pattern.md)

## Service Mesh Pattern

How to handle cross-service communication concerns (mTLS, retries, observability, routing) without coding them in every service.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/service-mesh-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/service-mesh-pattern.md)

values: playbook Architecture — TS / Node ≥22 / pnpm Monorepo (Concrete)

answer.markdown:
## Architecture — TS / Node ≥22 / pnpm Monorepo (Concrete)

Copy-paste-ready recipes that implement [`universal.md`](/docs/pillars/architecture/universal) on a TypeScript stack. Calibrated on a real multi-package, multi-app monorepo built primarily by AI agents over ~1 year.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/ts-concrete)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/ts-concrete.md)

## Architecture — Universal Principles

Stack-agnostic. Applies to any language, any framework.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/architecture/universal)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/architecture/universal.md)

## Pillar — Governance

How multiple agents (and humans) coordinate work in one repo without subtracting each other''s progress.

Type: **Pillar**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/governance)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/governance/index.md)

## Merge Rules Pattern

How to resolve conflicts so the merge sums work instead of subtracting it.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/governance/merge-rules-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/governance/merge-rules-pattern.md)

## Phased PR Pattern

How to ship initiatives too big for one PR without ending up with a long-lived branch hell.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/governance/phased-pr-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/governance/phased-pr-pattern.md)

## PR Intent Pattern

The manifest that makes a PR''s claims verifiable.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/governance/pr-intent-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/governance/pr-intent-pattern.md)

## Tombstone Pattern

How to retire a doc, plan, ADR, screen, or package without losing the trail.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/governance/tombstone-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/governance/tombstone-pattern.md)

## Governance — Universal Principles

How multiple contributors — agents and humans — coordinate so the whole sums.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/governance/universal)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/governance/universal.md)

## Adversarial Bug-Hunt Pattern

How to find real logic bugs with a find → refute → reproduce loop, instead of trusting a single agent that says "looks fine".

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/adversarial-bug-hunt-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/adversarial-bug-hunt-pattern.md)

## Agent Eval Framework Pattern

How to measure whether an AI agent is actually good — deterministic checks, LLM-as-judge, and production monitoring — so quality scales with the system instead of breaking as it grows.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/agent-eval-framework-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/agent-eval-framework-pattern.md)

## Alerting + Runbooks Pattern

How to turn observability data into a healthy alerting setup — pages that matter, runbooks that work, alert hygiene that prevents pager fatigue.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/alerting-runbooks-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/alerting-runbooks-pattern.md)

## Chaos Engineering Pattern

How to find failure modes before customers do — deliberately, in a controlled way.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/chaos-engineering-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/chaos-engineering-pattern.md)

## CI/CD Pipeline Pattern

How to wire continuous integration + continuous delivery so the path from commit to production is short, verifiable, reversible, and boring.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/ci-cd-pipeline-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/ci-cd-pipeline-pattern.md)

## Contract Testing Pattern

How to verify that consumer + provider agree on a contract, without expensive end-to-end tests.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/contract-testing-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/contract-testing-pattern.md)

## Cost Optimization Pattern (FinOps)

How to control cloud spend without micromanaging every commit.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/cost-optimization-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/cost-optimization-pattern.md)

## Fail-Loud Defaults Pattern

Why a silent no-op default plus a test harness that overrides it lets broken production wiring pass CI green.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/fail-loud-defaults-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/fail-loud-defaults-pattern.md)

## Pillar — Quality

How to know the code works without manually reviewing every agent-produced diff.

Type: **Pillar**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/index.md)

## Mutation Testing Pattern

How to score whether your unit tests actually catch bugs, beyond what coverage tells you.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/mutation-testing-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/mutation-testing-pattern.md)

## Observability Pattern

How to know what the system is doing in production, beyond ''tests passed''.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/observability-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/observability-pattern.md)

## Performance Budgets Pattern

How to keep ''it''s fast enough'' from drifting into ''why is it slow?''

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/performance-budgets-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/performance-budgets-pattern.md)

## Pre-push Pattern

The safety net between local changes and CI.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/pre-push-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/pre-push-pattern.md)

## Product Analytics + Experimentation Pattern

How to measure what users do, run experiments cleanly, and let data drive product decisions — without inviting bias or noise.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/product-analytics-experimentation-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/product-analytics-experimentation-pattern.md)

## Property-Based & Fuzz Testing Pattern

How to test the laws your code must obey, instead of guessing individual inputs — and how this catches crypto and parser bugs example tests never will.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/property-fuzz-testing-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/property-fuzz-testing-pattern.md)

## Quality Gates Pattern

How to bundle structural rules into one fast command an agent runs before every push.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/quality-gates-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/quality-gates-pattern.md)

## Sanity Pattern

The cross-cutting audit that catches what individual gates miss.

Type: **Playbook Pattern**

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/pillars/quality/sanity-pattern)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/pillars/quality/sanity-pattern.md)

## Templates

[Open the canonical guide](https://playbook.agentskit.io/docs/templates)
[Read raw Markdown](https://playbook.agentskit.io/raw/templates/index.md)
Start with the copy-ready onboarding prompt. It asks your coding agent to read the full bundle, audit the repository, rank gaps by risk, and wait for approval before changing code.
Fetch the complete LLM-readable corpus with `curl https://playbook.agentskit.io/llms-full.txt` or download `playbook-bundle.zip` for local indexing.
Contributions are welcome when a pattern is backed by a reproducible production lesson. Follow the contribution guide, include the failure mode and enforcement evidence, then open a pull request.
AgentsKit is the parent framework. Use it to build the agents and runtimes governed by Playbook patterns.
Use the Registry to install validated, readable agent source, then apply Playbook gates and review patterns as you adapt it.
AgentsKit Chat powers this local-first assistant and turns one behavior definition into native chat experiences across supported interfaces.
Doc Bridge validates the Playbook corpus, machine-readable index, ownership routes, and agent handoffs.
Use AgentsKit Code Review to run a focused, low-noise verification pass before an agent-authored change is merged.
Use AKOS when the system needs enterprise orchestration, governance, identity, audit, permissions, and production controls.
